terms.definition: Necrosis of heart muscle caused by sudden interruption of coronary blood supply.
terms.exampleSentence: The patient presented with crushing central chest pain and ECG changes consistent with an acute myocardial infarction.
terms.contextNotes: Often abbreviated STEMI or NSTEMI depending on ECG findings.

terms.definition: Chest pain or discomfort due to transient myocardial ischaemia, typically provoked by exertion.
terms.exampleSentence: Her stable angina pectoris was relieved within minutes of using a sublingual glyceryl trinitrate spray.
terms.contextNotes: Unstable angina is part of the acute coronary syndrome spectrum.

terms.definition: An irregular, often rapid supraventricular arrhythmia characterised by chaotic atrial electrical activity.
terms.exampleSentence: He was started on apixaban after newly diagnosed atrial fibrillation to reduce his risk of embolic stroke.
terms.contextNotes: Stroke risk is stratified using the CHA2DS2-VASc score.

terms.definition: An ultrasound investigation of cardiac structure and function.
terms.exampleSentence: A transthoracic echocardiogram demonstrated severely impaired left ventricular systolic function.
terms.contextNotes: Often shortened to 'echo' in handovers.

terms.definition: A clinical syndrome in which the heart cannot pump sufficient blood to meet metabolic demand.
terms.exampleSentence: She was admitted with decompensated heart failure and bilateral pleural effusions on chest X-ray.
collocations: heart failure with reduced ejection fraction
terms.contextNotes: Classified as HFrEF or HFpEF based on ejection fraction.

terms.definition: An invasive imaging procedure using contrast to visualise the coronary arteries.
terms.exampleSentence: Urgent coronary angiography revealed a critical proximal stenosis of the left anterior descending artery.
terms.contextNotes: Usually performed via radial or femoral arterial access.

terms.definition: A drug class that blocks beta-adrenergic receptors, reducing heart rate and myocardial workload.
terms.exampleSentence: Bisoprolol, a cardioselective beta-blocker, was titrated up to manage her chronic heart failure.
terms.contextNotes: Use cautiously in asthma due to risk of bronchospasm.

terms.definition: Persistently elevated arterial blood pressure above accepted clinical thresholds.
terms.exampleSentence: His long-standing hypertension had contributed to left ventricular hypertrophy on echocardiography.
terms.contextNotes: NICE recommends ABPM to confirm diagnosis above 140/90 mmHg in clinic.

terms.definition: A small expandable mesh tube inserted to keep a narrowed or blocked vessel patent.
terms.exampleSentence: A drug-eluting stent was deployed across the lesion in the right coronary artery.
terms.contextNotes: Patients require dual antiplatelet therapy after deployment.

terms.definition: The thick-walled lower chamber of the heart that pumps oxygenated blood into the systemic circulation.
terms.exampleSentence: The echocardiogram showed dilatation of the left ventricle with global hypokinesia.
terms.contextNotes: LVEF below 40% defines reduced ejection fraction heart failure.

terms.definition: An abnormal awareness of one's own heartbeat, often described as fluttering, pounding or racing.
terms.exampleSentence: She described intermittent palpitations associated with light-headedness over the preceding week.
terms.contextNotes: Investigate with ECG and ambulatory monitoring.

terms.definition: A drug class that blocks angiotensin-converting enzyme to reduce blood pressure and cardiac afterload.
terms.exampleSentence: Ramipril, an ACE inhibitor, was started cautiously due to her borderline renal function.
terms.contextNotes: Monitor renal function and potassium after initiation.

terms.definition: A regulatory cardiac muscle protein measured in serum to detect myocardial injury.
terms.exampleSentence: A markedly elevated high-sensitivity troponin confirmed the diagnosis of acute myocardial infarction.
terms.contextNotes: Serial measurements help distinguish acute injury from chronic elevation.

terms.definition: Formation of a blood clot within a deep vein, most commonly in the lower limb.
terms.exampleSentence: A Doppler ultrasound confirmed deep vein thrombosis in the right popliteal vein.
terms.contextNotes: Wells score guides pre-test probability assessment.

terms.definition: A resting heart rate below 60 beats per minute in adults.
terms.exampleSentence: Symptomatic bradycardia of 38 beats per minute prompted urgent referral for permanent pacing.
terms.contextNotes: Common in athletes and may be physiological.

terms.definition: A chronic inflammatory airway disorder causing reversible bronchoconstriction and variable airflow obstruction.
terms.exampleSentence: Her childhood asthma flared during the pollen season despite regular inhaled corticosteroid therapy.
terms.contextNotes: Step up therapy according to BTS/SIGN or NICE guidelines.

terms.definition: A progressive airflow-limiting respiratory disorder usually caused by long-term tobacco exposure.
terms.exampleSentence: Spirometry confirmed chronic obstructive pulmonary disease with an FEV1 of 45% predicted.
terms.contextNotes: Severity is graded by post-bronchodilator FEV1 percentage predicted.

terms.definition: Acute infection of the lung parenchyma causing consolidation and impaired gas exchange.
terms.exampleSentence: Right lower lobe pneumonia was confirmed on chest X-ray and treated with intravenous co-amoxiclav.
terms.contextNotes: Use CURB-65 score to assess severity and admission need.

terms.definition: Obstruction of a pulmonary artery, usually by a thromboembolus from the deep venous system.
terms.exampleSentence: CT pulmonary angiography revealed a large saddle pulmonary embolism causing right heart strain.
terms.contextNotes: Haemodynamically unstable PE may warrant systemic thrombolysis.

terms.definition: The subjective sensation of breathing difficulty or uncomfortable awareness of breathing.
terms.exampleSentence: He reported worsening exertional dyspnoea over the past three months and orthopnoea at night.
terms.contextNotes: Quantify using the MRC Dyspnoea Scale in chronic respiratory disease.

terms.definition: Coughing up blood originating from the lower respiratory tract.
terms.exampleSentence: Persistent haemoptysis in a long-term smoker prompted urgent two-week-wait referral for suspected lung cancer.
terms.contextNotes: Differentiate from haematemesis and epistaxis at the bedside.

terms.definition: A pulmonary function test measuring the volume and rate of air exhaled and inhaled.
terms.exampleSentence: Post-bronchodilator spirometry demonstrated an FEV1/FVC ratio below 0.7, confirming airflow obstruction.
terms.contextNotes: Essential for diagnosing and grading COPD severity.

terms.definition: A short-acting beta-2 agonist bronchodilator used to relieve acute bronchospasm.
terms.exampleSentence: Nebulised salbutamol provided rapid symptomatic relief during her acute asthma attack.
terms.contextNotes: Known as albuterol in North American practice.

terms.definition: Endoscopic visualisation of the tracheobronchial tree, often with biopsy or lavage.
terms.exampleSentence: Flexible bronchoscopy was performed to obtain biopsies from the right upper lobe lesion.
terms.contextNotes: Often combined with EBUS for sampling mediastinal lymph nodes.

terms.definition: One of the major airways branching from the trachea to the lungs.
terms.exampleSentence: The inhaled foreign body had lodged in the right main bronchus due to its more vertical orientation.
terms.contextNotes: Plural is bronchi; right main bronchus is wider and more vertical.

terms.definition: Non-invasive measurement of peripheral arterial oxygen saturation using a fingertip probe.
terms.exampleSentence: Pulse oximetry on room air recorded saturations of 88%, prompting controlled oxygen therapy.
terms.contextNotes: May be unreliable in poor perfusion or carbon monoxide poisoning.

terms.definition: An abnormal accumulation of fluid within the pleural cavity surrounding the lungs.
terms.exampleSentence: A large left-sided pleural effusion was drained via ultrasound-guided thoracocentesis.
terms.contextNotes: Light's criteria classify fluid as transudate or exudate.

terms.definition: A device that converts liquid medication into a fine aerosol mist for inhalation.
terms.exampleSentence: She received back-to-back nebulisers of salbutamol and ipratropium during her exacerbation.
terms.contextNotes: Drive with air rather than oxygen in chronic CO2 retainers.

terms.definition: A chronic granulomatous infection caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis, typically affecting the lungs.
terms.exampleSentence: Sputum smears were positive for acid-fast bacilli, confirming pulmonary tuberculosis.
terms.contextNotes: Standard regimen is six months of RIPE therapy.

terms.definition: An abnormally low partial pressure of oxygen in arterial blood.
terms.exampleSentence: Persistent hypoxaemia despite high-flow oxygen prompted escalation to non-invasive ventilation.
terms.contextNotes: Distinguish from hypoxia, which refers to tissue oxygen deficit.
